_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to match the appropriate knowledge repository technology for a given application or environment.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select appropriate technical tools, languages, and platforms for data and AI requirements, evaluating trade-offs between performance, maintainability, team capability, ecosystem maturity, and cost.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-K-006 (AB, cosine: 0.554), DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.553).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.493). Shared vocabulary: [ability, appropriate]. DCWF-only terms [application, environment, given, knowledge, match, repository] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, capability, cost, data, ecosystem, evaluating] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.881 (P=0.905, R=0.858). P exceeds R by 0.047 — the DCWF element's 14 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Ability to match the appropriate knowledge repository technology for a given app" and "Ability to select appropriate technical tools, languages, and platforms for data" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.558 > STS 0.493 (gap +0.065). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ability, appropriate] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.8%. Shared tokens: [ability, appropriate]. DCWF-only: [application, environment, given, knowledge, match, repository]. WIDAI-only: [ai, capability, cost, data, ecosystem, evaluating].

signals.detail: STS 0.493 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
